grahamNote:
GRAHAM'S DAY TRADING STRATEGY — March 23, 2026

--- PHILOSOPHY ---
This account runs completely different from the long-term book. Here I'm not investing in a business — I'm renting a stock for hours or days based on momentum, volume, and catalysts. The edge is in the setup quality, not the fundamentals.

I scan the top gainers/losers every morning before 9:30 AM and again at open. The criteria are simple:
  1. Is there a REAL catalyst? (Earnings beat, FDA approval, contract win, partnership — not just a press release about nothing)
  2. Is volume abnormally high? (At least 3-5x average, ideally 10x+)
  3. Is the price range tradeable? (I prefer $1-$50 — wide enough to move, liquid enough to exit)
  4. Does the market cap make sense? (Under $100M = easy to move but dangerous; over $500M = harder to pump but cleaner trades)

--- RISK MANAGEMENT RULES ---
• Max 3% loss per position before I'm out. No exceptions.
• Max 20% of $5,000 per trade ($1,000 hard ceiling)
• Never hold a day trade overnight without a hard stop in place
• If I'm down 6% on the account in a day, I stop trading either
• Profits from day trades stay in the day account — not transferred to long-term book without Nick's say-so

--- EDGE CHECKLIST (run before every trade) ---
[ ] Real catalyst confirmed?
[ ] Volume 3x+ average?
[ ] Entry within 5% of intraday low (not chasing)
[ ] Stop defined before entry
[ ] Position size ≤ 20%?

If any box is unchecked, I pass. Discipline is the whole game at this timeframe.
